legal_basis: Wet op de internationale bijstandsverlening bij de heffing van belastingen (WIB); amended 1 January 2016 to incorporate CRS; EU Directive 2014/107/EU (DAC2) transposed
crs_status: Committed and active - first exchange 2017 (early adopter)
reporting_deadline: 30 June following the 31 December reporting year-end [INFERRED - verify with Belastingdienst announcement]
portal_name: Digipoort (Dutch electronic gateway for business tax submissions)
submission_method: Digipoort electronic gateway; XML upload in OECD CRS XML format; PKI-certificate required for authentication
xml_schema: OECD CRS XML Schema v2.0; DAC2 XML for EU exchanges
nil_report_required: Yes - nil report required under Dutch WIB [INFERRED - verify with Belastingdienst]
data_retention_years: 7 years (Algemene wet inzake rijksbelastingen art. 52; standard Dutch fiscal record retention)
msg_ref_id_format: NL + FI RSIN (Rechtspersonen en Samenwerkingsverbanden Informatienummer) + year + sequence
doc_ref_id_format: NL + year + FI RSIN + unique account reference
local_fi_scope: Dutch banks (kredietinstellingen) under Wft (Wet op het financieel toezicht) supervised by DNB
Dutch investment firms (beleggingsondernemingen) supervised by AFM
Dutch life insurance companies (levensverzekeraars) with beleggingsverzekeringen (investment-linked)
Dutch investment funds (beleggingsinstellingen): UCITS (icbe) and AIFs supervised by AFM
Dutch branches of foreign FIs resident in Netherlands for CRS purposes
excluded_accounts: Qualified credit card issuer accounts (where applicable)
Lijfrente accounts (annuity savings) specifically qualifying as pension - verify exclusion with Belastingdienst
Dutch state pension (AOW) entitlements - governmental, excluded
local_specific_rules: DIGIPOORT AND PKI CERTIFICATE: Dutch CRS submissions go via Digipoort, the Dutch government's secure electronic gateway. FIs must have a valid PKI-Overheid certificate (Dutch government PKI) to authenticate. Obtaining and renewing PKI certificates requires lead time; plan well in advance.

RSIN AS FI IDENTIFIER: The RSIN (Rechtspersonen en Samenwerkingsverbanden Informatienummer, 9 digits) is the primary identifier for Dutch legal entities in tax matters. It is used in DocRefId and MessageRefId construction.
DAC2 OVERLAY: Netherlands as EU member applies EU DAC2 Directive for exchanges with EU member states. For non-EU CRS partners, MCAA applies. In practice, the XML reporting format is the same.

DNB/AFM SUPERVISION: De Nederlandsche Bank (DNB) supervises Dutch banks and insurers from a prudential perspective. AFM supervises investment firms. Belastingdienst supervises CRS data reporting. FIs may be inspected by multiple regulators.

SELF-CERTIFICATION (VERKLARING FISCAAL WOON-/VESTIGINGSLAND): Account holders must provide a 'verklaring' (declaration) of their fiscal residence country when asked by the FI. Dutch financial institutions typically have this embedded in account opening forms.
DUTCH PENSION EXCLUSION: Most Dutch pension funds (pensioenfondsen) qualify as Non-Reporting FIs. However, FIs managing assets FOR pension funds must still classify those fund accounts correctly.

EU BENEFICIAL OWNER REGISTER: Dutch FIs should cross-reference their CRS self-cert procedures with UBO (Ultimate Beneficial Owner) register obligations under Dutch AML law, as the information overlaps.
WEBPAGE LAST MODIFIED: The official Belastingdienst CRS page was last modified 22 January 2026 per page metadata.
LANGUAGE: Official Belastingdienst CRS guidance is in Dutch (Nederlands) only. English sub-pages exist for general tax information but the CRS-specific sub-pages are Dutch-only.

rejection_error_codes.resolution: Renew PKI-Overheid certificate; check Digipoort authentication settings
Register FI with Belastingdienst for CRS obligations
Validate against OECD CRS XSD; check Belastingdienst technical specifications
Use OECD2 with CorrDocRefId to amend

penalties.failure_to_report: Under WIB / AWR: administrative fine up to EUR 8,200 per violation (standard AWR maximum); higher for deliberate non-compliance
penalties.late_submission: Administrative fine; Belastingdienst may issue compliance notice

contact.notes: Dutch-language guidance only for CRS. English sub-pages available for general info.

fatca.portal: Digipoort (same as CRS; separate FATCA XML)
key_rules: Netherlands Model 1A: Dutch FIs report FATCA data to Belastingdienst, which passes to IRS.
FATCA reporting via Digipoort alongside CRS.
Dutch FIs do not register as FFIs on IRS portal; covered by IGA.

sources.notes: Belastingdienst CRS main page - fetched and verified. Page modified 22 Jan 2026.

gaps: Reporting deadline (30 June) is INFERRED; verify with Belastingdienst annual announcement
Digipoort technical submission specifications URL should be added
Penalty amounts should be confirmed against current WIB / AWR provisions
Dutch-specific XML technical requirements should be obtained from Belastingdienst
